# ADR-004: EmbeddedImage Renderer is Opt-In

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The default `EmbeddedAssetBlock` renderer renders a placeholder string (empty or entry-reference text) for all embedded asset blocks. In v3.3.0, an `EmbeddedImage` renderer was added that checks if an embedded asset is an image and, if so, renders a proper `<img>` tag.

Enabling this by default would be a breaking change: existing applications that had custom renderers for `EmbeddedAssetBlock` (or expected the old empty/string output) would see different HTML output without any code changes on their side.

## Decision

The `EmbeddedImage` renderer is not registered in `Renderer::createNodeRenderers()`. It must be explicitly enabled via:

```php
$renderer->enableEmbeddedImageRenderer(true);
```

This method pushes the `EmbeddedImage` instance onto the renderer stack (making it highest-priority for `EmbeddedAssetBlock` nodes that contain image assets). It can also be disabled by calling with `false`, which removes it from the stack.

Because `EmbeddedImage` supports only `EmbeddedAssetBlock` nodes where the asset is an image file (checked via `method_exists` duck-typing on `getFile()` and `getTitle()`), it safely falls through to the next renderer for non-image assets.

## Consequences

- Existing applications are unaffected by the `EmbeddedImage` renderer — no behavior change unless they opt in.
- Applications that want automatic `<img>` tag rendering call one method to enable it.
- The `method_exists()` duck-typing in `EmbeddedImage::supports()` is a deliberate trade-off: `AssetInterface` does not expose `getFile()` or `getTitle()` (adding them would be a BC break), so runtime checks are used instead. This is documented with `@phpstan-ignore-line` comments.
- Subclasses of `EmbeddedImage` can override `renderImage()` to customize the `<img>` output without reimplementing the full supports/render logic.
